page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the DSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The instance features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Vector Neural Network Instructions, which contribute to top-tier performance in cryptographic operations, as evidenced by a top 10% Passmark CPU encryption score. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, with uncached read speeds reaching 323.4 GB/sec and write speeds at 131.45 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%.

While multi-threaded execution and memory throughput are strong, single-core performance remains in the average tier. This performance profile is reflected in large language model inference benchmarks, where prompt processing is moderately fast, but text generation for large 70B models falls into the bottom 25% due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Consequently, this instance is highly suitable for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, such as static web serving, Redis databases, software compilation, and general-purpose application hosting, rather than single-threaded tasks or heavy LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores, 384.0 GB of system memory, and no local storage or GPUs. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, ranking in the top 25% across multiple benchmarks. It excels in cryptographic tasks, achieving top-tier encryption scores due to AVX-512 support. However, single-core performance is average, and large-scale LLM text generation is weak without GPU acceleration. This instance is best suited for highly parallelized workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallelized enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity, multi-core virtual machine delivering balanced memory and compute resources for parallelized enterprise applications and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 384.0 GB of RAM
Strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in the top 25% of benchmarks
Top-tier cryptographic execution supported by AVX-512 instruction sets
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
